hard.next_step: Confidence 75% 3rd Jul- End of July customer will be concluded all evaluations for the competition from Demo point of view our position it was good and some tech queries need response from our team , additionally we need to provide revised quotes via KPMG and Accenture and KPMG as per customer request . 23rd of June 2026 - scheduled for live Demo SRM , and we will discuss our position with the RFP and will discuss SIs and DC . 6th Jun Ongoing clarifications with advisor (Franck) , references, DC, info Sec etc 21st May- Franck reached out to get clarification on "on prem vs cloud" response in our RFP- ck has responded 13th May - RFP Clarifications (~600) submitted and receipt clarification received .... considering the complexity expecting this will be an iterative process. 4th May- RFP submitted- clarification questions now received (extensive)- asked for extension to respond- accepted request. Significant effort required to respond with utmost accuracy 25th Mar- RFP extension advised- closing April 21st (EY has agreed to prime and supply 30% local Saudi resources, as required) 13th Mar- RFP is now out and under review by team- 2 weeks to seek clarifications and address internal pros/cons 2nd Mar Regional military disruption in region- however SAMI have confirmed Zycus is shortlisted (confidentially, to 3) RFP to come out in next 1 week All further meetings to be approved by CK in ME 26th Feb- Positive presentation/workshop (4 hours). Process is better defined- currently evaluating internally (Zycus team) on definitive fit Team to huddle this week and work through next step strategies. 3 vendors in mix, all will be similar fit to Zycus- we need edge here- and currently investigating how we can drive a strong AI message and solution 10th Feb- good interaction with team- demo confirmed 23rd Feb (STE is pivotal) 30th Jan Updated from SAMI (Franck)- we are invited to do presentations (they call Innovation Contest) week - 22nf Feb-26th Feb Details/expectations of demos due next 2 days. 24th Jan - RFI submitted, next steps live demos early Feb. 22nd Jan - Clarifications received a week late, working on the RFI response due Saturday 13th Jan ck met with SAMI team F2F- very positive intro and overview focused on STE- current FRO out and responding by 20th Jan 22nd Dec - Confirmed participation, next step is clarifications (deadline 8th Jan) and RFI build - some big gaps, but could be doable with PNG support. 16th Dec - RFI received for full S2P - deadline is 24th Jan , Carl also reached out and arranged a ST Engineering overview on the 13th. 15th Dec - NDA received by Viraat (Alliances) in order to receive an RFI for 'SRM' - signed and sent with follow up highlighting ST Engineering Confirm Zycus participation in SAMI SRM RFI by 22 Dec 2025 , raise SFDC opportunity, initiate cross-functional RFI response team, and begin review of RFI requirements and compliance mapping ahead of Jan 2026 submission milestones.

forecast_read.reason: Best Case is claimed before SAMI has decided: the award decision lands in a final round around mid-July 2026 (Franck Devienne, 23 Jun 2026 demo) inside a live three-vendor bake-off with no champion and an arms-length economic buyer. The deal itself is healthy and engaged (buyer chased the final proposal 7 Jul 2026), so this is a date and category problem, not a deal problem: if SAMI selects Zycus in late July, the 29 Aug 2026 close leaves roughly four weeks for SI-mediated commercials, defense InfoSec and contracting on a $2.2M on-premise A&D deployment.
forecast_read.math: 51 days to close (2026-08-29) as of 2026-07-09. In Shortlisted since the confidential down-select to 3 on 2026-03-02 (129 days in stage). Close date already slipped once, 2026-06-29 to 2026-08-29 on 2026-06-07. Buyer-stated pace: final decision round mid-July 2026, evaluations concluded end of July 2026, leaving about 4 weeks post-award to paper a $2.2M defense deal by 29 Aug.

metrics.narrative: No buyer-quantified figures (cost, cycle time, savings) have surfaced on any call; the pain case is qualitative. MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-05-15) frames it as fragmented, manual supplier-facing processes across HOLDCO/OPCOs and the need for one auditable platform to support direct/defense procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP. Franck Devienne stated on 13 Jan 2026 that selection will be on future-need fulfilment, not commercials, which lowers the pressure for a hard ROI case but leaves the value case unquantified going into the mid-July 2026 decision.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15) names Franck Devienne, Senior Procurement Advisor, as economic buyer, with budget 'centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision.' He visibly controls the process: he ran the 23 Jun 2026 demo agenda, set the decision timeline ('a final round of discussions around mid July when we'll make the decisions'), and invited commercial questions directly ('any question, commercial question whatsoever, reach me out today by e mail'). But no CFO, CPO or board-level sponsor has appeared on any call or email through 7 Jul 2026, and the same CRM record flags 'a very arms-length process,' so true budget authority above Franck remains unconfirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are documented and were tested live: functional/technical compliance, cybersecurity, SAP S/4HANA integration, A&D/direct-procurement/MRO fit, local content/LCGPA/SDP/IPP fit, KSA implementation capability, knowledge transfer, a filled compliance matrix and the commercial response (SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c, 15 May 2026). June hardened three of them: hosting must be on-premise at AEC, a SAMI subsidiary (Franck Devienne, 16 Jun 2026: 'hosting at AEC would be considered as on-premise'); SAMI will select the implementor together with the solution (Franck, 19 Jun 2026); and core functions like historical price visibility must work with AI disabled (Franck at the 23 Jun 2026 demo: 'Consider it is disabled').

decision_process.narrative: A clean, traceable sequence: NDA (15 Dec 2025), RFI (16 Dec 2025, submitted 24 Jan 2026), Innovation Contest presentations (22-26 Feb 2026), confidential down-select to 3 vendors (2 Mar 2026), RFP issued 13 Mar 2026 and submitted 4 May 2026 with ~600 clarifications answered by 13 May 2026, identical-agenda vendor demos the week of 21-23 Jun 2026, then a final round of discussions around mid-July 2026 with all evaluations concluded by end of July (Franck Devienne, 23 Jun 2026; corroborated 3 Jul 2026). Franck stated no decision would come in the two to three weeks after the demo and that silence means nothing. The award covers both the SRM solution and its implementor, run concurrently with SAMI's SAP rollout in its OPCOs.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, legal or formal InfoSec artifacts have surfaced; the deal is still in evaluation. Three structural signals will shape the eventual paper: commercial quotes must route through SI partners (KPMG, EY, with Accenture requested) per SAMI's 19 Jun 2026 instruction; hosting is on-premise at AEC, a SAMI subsidiary, which adds a deployment and data-center layer to contracting; and defense-grade compliance (FARS, classified-material handling) will sit inside any paper. What contracting vehicle SAMI intends post-award (direct MSA vs SI-mediated framework) has not been asked or answered.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated in MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15): fragmented and manual supplier-facing processes spanning onboarding, qualification, sourcing and development; lack of transparency and standardized supplier data across SAMI's HOLDCO/OPCO structure; and the need to support direct/defense procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP in one auditable platform. Franck Devienne confirmed the greenfield reality on 13 Jan 2026 ('Today, we have nothing') as SAMI builds procurement alongside its new SAP ERP. The pain owner is the SAMI procurement function (Franck Devienne's team).

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15) is explicit: 'Very arms-length process - Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role.' The 23 Jun 2026 demo confirmed the posture: Franck Devienne ran a deliberately vendor-neutral process ('same agenda was sent to actually everyone') and warned that post-demo silence means nothing. Yousef Alarabi actively drives completion (chased the final proposal 7 Jul 2026) but that is process diligence, not advocacy. No internal introduction, shared internal document, or pro-Zycus push against a rival has been evidenced on any call.

competition.narrative: Four named rivals: SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP, Coupa (SF competitor field; corroborated by MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026). SAMI confirmed a confidential down-select to 3 on 2 Mar 2026 and ran identical-agenda demos across those finalists the week of 21-23 Jun 2026 (Franck Devienne: 'we are requesting the different vendors... same agenda was sent to actually everyone'), though the rival finalists are never named. SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat: SAMI runs S/4HANA and is rolling it out across OPCOs with Accenture and KPMG (Franck email, 19 Jun 2026), and buyers probe vendors on 'why not Ariba' (MEDDPICC 2.0). Zycus's counters are the ST Engineering A&D reference with concurrent S/4 implementation, the demonstrated local-content/GAMI dashboard (23 Jun 2026), and a Riyadh-based team, activated only if the open compliance and integration responses land before the decision.

critical_signals.text: SAMI chased Zycus's final proposal on 7 Jul with its award decision due mid-to-late July; the KPMG and EY implementation quotes were still being finalized and Accenture's remained unconfirmed.

The 23 Jun live SRM demo ran SAMI's full agenda for 2.5 hours before a cross-functional panel including its local-content team, and Franck Devienne confirmed a final decision round around mid-July with evaluations concluded by end of July.
SAMI ran identical-agenda demos across its final three vendors while rolling out SAP S/4HANA in its OPCOs with Accenture and KPMG, a backdrop that favors SAP Ariba as the low-risk default.

Hosting must sit on-premise at AEC, a SAMI subsidiary, and SAMI will select the implementor together with the solution, so Zycus's bid must arrive bundled with KPMG, EY and Accenture partner proposals.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is a confirmed finalist in a live, tightly run three-vendor evaluation: shortlisted 2 Mar 2026, RFP plus ~600 clarifications completed by 13 May 2026, and a 2.5-hour buyer-agenda demo delivered 23 Jun 2026. Buyer pull is real (SAMI chased the final proposal on 7 Jul 2026) and the decision lands mid-to-late July 2026. What caps the position: no champion, an arms-length economic buyer, unanswered defense-compliance questions, and a proposal package that was still landing a week before the decision round.

win_position.text: SAMI confidentially confirmed Zycus into its final three on 2 Mar 2026 and has since invested heavily in evaluating Zycus: ~600 RFP clarifications processed by 13 May 2026 and a 2.5-hour cross-functional demo on 23 Jun 2026.

Buyer-side pull continued to 7 Jul 2026, when Yousef Alarabi wrote 'We are still waiting for your final proposal,' and Franck Devienne personally chased the AEC-hosting proposal and partner quotes through late June.

No champion exists (CRM, 15 May 2026: Franck 'doesn't fit the champion role') and no executive above Franck Devienne has appeared on any call or email, so the award ratification path is invisible to Zycus.

SAP Ariba is the structural default: SAMI is rolling out S/4HANA across its OPCOs with Accenture and KPMG (Franck email, 19 Jun 2026), and Zycus's written defense-compliance answers from the 23 Jun demo were still open on 3 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer initiated the demo round (16 Jun 2026 invitation), fixed concrete dates (final round mid-July, evaluations concluded end of July), and sent six chasing emails between 16 Jun and 7 Jul 2026.
Commercial topics are live: SAMI requested revised quotes via KPMG and Accenture (19 Jun 2026) and Franck invited direct commercial questions by email at the 23 Jun demo.
All remaining motion is Zycus-owed: the final proposal, SI quotes and written technical responses were the open items as of 7 Jul 2026, two weeks after they were promised.

customer_commitment.text: SAMI committed a cross-functional panel (procurement, evaluators, the local-content team) for 2.5 hours on 23 Jun 2026 and had its evaluators pose defense-specific tests (FARS, classified materials, consignment stock with S/4HANA MRP).
The commitment is to the process, not to Zycus: Franck Devienne ran the same agenda for every vendor and stated on 23 Jun 2026 that silence before the mid-July round means nothing.

deal_risk.text: If SAMI selects Zycus in its mid-to-late July 2026 round, only about four weeks remain to the 29 Aug close for SI-mediated commercials, defense InfoSec and contracting; the date, more than the deal, is at risk.

Zycus deliverables sat open through the decision run-up: written answers to Ahmed Almania's compliance questions (promised 23 Jun 2026) and the SI-routed quotes Carl committed for end of the week of 22 Jun 2026 were still incomplete on 7 Jul 2026.

Single-thread exposure: every SAMI interaction routes through Franck Devienne and Yousef Alarabi at procurement level on a $2.2M award that SAMI's own record says is decided by 'Franck and team' with budget held centrally.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for any vendor. Franck Devienne kept the 23 Jun 2026 demo deliberately neutral ('same agenda was sent to actually everyone') and stated that hearing nothing before the mid-July decision round 'doesn't mean anything.' The only positive read on Zycus's standing ('our position it was good,' 3 Jul 2026) is the rep's, not the buyer's.

business_case.evidence: Pain and platform need are confirmed (SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c, 15 May 2026: fragmented manual supplier processes across HOLDCO/OPCOs, local-content and defense-procurement obligations), and Franck Devienne said on 13 Jan 2026 that selection rests on future-need fulfilment rather than commercials, but no quantified value case (savings, cycle time, compliance-risk figures) has been built with the buyer.

deal_movement.summary: Since mid-June the deal moved from RFP clarifications into the demo round and the final evaluation run-up: SAMI invited and received the 23 Jun 2026 SRM demo, fixed its decision timeline (final round mid-July, evaluations concluded end of July), hardened two structural requirements (on-premise hosting at AEC; implementor selected with the solution), and by 7 Jul 2026 was chasing Zycus's final proposal.

items.change: Close date moved from 2026-06-29 to 2026-08-29 (field history, Carl Kimball).
SAMI invited Zycus to the SRM demo round and classified AEC hosting as on-premise ('AEC is (i) a defense company, and (ii) a SAMI subsidiary').
SAMI confirmed it will select the implementor together with the solution and requested Accenture and KPMG proposals as alternatives to EY.
Live 2.5-hour SRM demo delivered to a cross-functional SAMI panel; Franck Devienne set a final decision round for mid-July.
Zycus sent the revised data-center option offering the AEC data warehouse (slide 5 update).
Yousef Alarabi chased the final proposal; Carl Kimball confirmed the KPMG and EY SI proposals nearly complete and the updated proposal list already sent.

day_summary.overall: Yousef Alarabi wrote that SAMI is still waiting for Zycus's final proposal, a chase landing days before SAMI's own mid-July decision round. Carl Kimball replied the same day that the implementation-partner proposals with KPMG and EY are nearly complete and that the updated proposal list had already been sent. The evaluation channel is live and the remaining blocker is Zycus-side delivery.

items.name: SAMI chases the final proposal
items.summary: Yousef Alarabi asked when Zycus's final proposal will arrive, noting SAMI is still waiting.

items.name: Zycus responds on SI proposals
items.summary: Carl Kimball confirmed the KPMG and EY implementation proposals are nearly complete and the updated proposal list was already sent.

competitive_position.summary: A live, formal bake-off: SAMI confirmed a confidential down-select to 3 vendors on 2 Mar 2026 and ran identical-agenda SRM demos across the finalists the week of 21-23 Jun 2026 (Franck Devienne: 'same agenda was sent to actually everyone'); the rival finalists are never named in any source. SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: SAMI runs S/4HANA and is rolling it out across its OPCOs with Accenture and KPMG (19 Jun 2026), making the SAP-native suite the low-risk default, and buyers already probe vendors on 'why not Ariba' (MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026). Ivalua (heavy customization and localization) and GEP (heavy services support) are live secondary threats; Coupa is the lowest current threat with no fresh 2026 traction at SAMI. Zycus's counters: the ST Engineering A&D reference with concurrent S/4HANA implementation, the local-content/GAMI dashboard demonstrated 23 Jun 2026, iSaaS adapters, and a Riyadh-based sales and implementation team.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba (relationship with SAP/IBM - but they ask for detail on why nor Ariba, clearly they are unliked)
competitors.source: SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c (2026-05-15); SAMI S/4HANA OPCO rollout with Accenture and KPMG per Franck Devienne email, 19 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Land the promised written integration and compliance answers (consignment stock with S/4HANA MRP, FARS) before the mid-July 2026 decision round, and lead the final proposal with the ST Engineering concurrent-S/4 implementation proof and iSaaS adapter evidence to remove the 'Ariba is lower risk on SAP' fallback.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.how_we_win: Position the configurable-not-customized local-content and GAMI dashboards demonstrated on 23 Jun 2026 as a lower-risk localization path than heavy customization.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.how_we_win: Counter the services-led pitch with the single-codebase platform, the Riyadh-based implementation team announced at the 23 Jun 2026 demo, and the SAMI-chosen SI model (KPMG, EY, Accenture) that gives SAMI its preferred implementor.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: No fresh 2026 evidence of active Coupa traction at SAMI; monitor for resurgence but do not over-invest here.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: SAMI's evaluation tests defense-grade constraints: on-premise hosting at its AEC subsidiary, trade and FARS compliance, S/4HANA integration, AI-optional core functions, and local-content reporting. Zycus is aligned on local content and has resolved the hosting model, but the written compliance answers and the historical-price-without-AI confirmation were still open as of 3 Jul 2026.

best_practice_check.summary: The evaluation is won or lost in the next two weeks on delivery completeness, access above procurement, and neutralizing the SAP-native default; the champion gap cannot be fixed before the decision, so the play is flawless process execution plus a first executive thread.
flags: Use the commercial channel Franck Devienne explicitly opened at the 23 Jun 2026 demo ('any commercial question whatsoever, reach me out today by e mail') to pre-frame pricing structure before the SI-mediated quotes land, keeping Zycus in control of the commercial narrative.

Multi-thread above procurement: every interaction runs through Franck Devienne and Yousef Alarabi; use Mohamad Alhakim's Riyadh presence and the AEC relationship (Muhammad Ali) to reach the executives who ratify a $2.2M award.

Neutralize the SAP Ariba default: SAMI's concurrent S/4HANA rollout with Accenture and KPMG favors the SAP-native suite, so lead the final proposal with the ST Engineering concurrent-S/4 implementation proof, iSaaS adapter evidence, and the answered consignment/MRP integration question.

champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Franck Devienne, the closest recurring contact, is explicitly flagged in the CRM as not fitting the champion role and ran the 23 Jun 2026 demo on a deliberately vendor-neutral basis. No SAMI insider has evidenced advocacy for Zycus (no internal introduction, no shared internal document, no pro-Zycus pushback) through 7 Jul 2026.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won opportunity at SAMI is in evidence; executive access must be built fresh. ST Engineering is an external reference customer, not a SAMI relationship.

scope_change.detail: Scope and amount unchanged since spring. Background: Amount was raised from 1,200,000 to 2,200,000 and the forecast moved Pipeline to Best Case on 25 Mar 2026 (field history, Carl Kimball) as the full-S2P RFP scope firmed with EY primed to supply 30% local Saudi resources.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: SAMI has explicitly de-prioritized AI for this cycle: on 13 Jan 2026 Franck Devienne called agentic AI 'way beyond our maturity' and not a key criterion, and at the 23 Jun 2026 demo he checked that search works without AI, insisted historical price visibility function with AI off ('Consider it is disabled'), and noted SAMI runs Zoom on-prem with no Microsoft Teams, forcing Merlin Intake into a URL-only posture. Zycus's AI depth (Merlin, ANA, intake) remains a differentiator held in reserve for SAMI's stated future, but the winning positioning now is AI-optional compliance with on-premise constraints, not an AI-led message.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Resistant for this procurement cycle: SAMI requires on-premise hosting at its AEC subsidiary, prohibits external data access for AI today ('today, we are not even considering' it, Franck Devienne, 13 Jan 2026), and asks vendors to treat AI features as disabled. The resistance is maturity-staged, not ideological: Franck acknowledged the industry direction and framed a learn-to-walk-then-fly path, so AI is a post-implementation expansion story.

items.detail: The 29 Aug 2026 close allows only about four weeks after SAMI's mid-to-late July decision for SI-mediated commercials, defense InfoSec and contracting; the date already slipped once (29 Jun to 29 Aug on 7 Jun 2026).
items.source: OpportunityFieldHistory (CloseDate, 7 Jun 2026); Franck Devienne decision timeline, 23 Jun 2026

items.detail: No champion and no engaged executive: the CRM flags a 'very arms-length process,' Franck Devienne does not fit the champion role, and no one above procurement has appeared on any interaction through 7 Jul 2026.
items.source: SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c (2026-05-15); activity record Dec 2025-Jul 2026

items.detail: Defense-compliance questions (FARS, trade compliance, classified-material segregation, consignment stock with S/4HANA MRP) have been open since before the 23 Jun 2026 demo, with written answers promised post-session and still outstanding on 3 Jul 2026; SAMI's cybersecurity rules also bar third-party API validation in the on-premise model.
items.source: 23 Jun 2026 demo transcript (Ahmed Almania; Franck Devienne on cybersecurity); Next_Step__c 3 Jul 2026

items.detail: SAMI selects the implementor with the solution and asked for Accenture and KPMG proposals alongside EY (19 Jun 2026). As of 7 Jul 2026 Carl Kimball named only KPMG and EY as nearly complete; Accenture, SAMI's own SAP rollout partner, has not been confirmed as proposing with Zycus.
items.source: Franck Devienne email, 19 Jun 2026; Carl Kimball emails 19 Jun and 7 Jul 2026

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and buyer-pulled: SAMI is chasing Zycus deliverables into its own decision window, which is the opposite of cooling. The confidence ceiling is structural (neutral process, no champion), not behavioral.
items: Yousef Alarabi chased Zycus's final proposal on 7 Jul 2026, pulling Zycus toward the decision rather than going quiet.
SAMI volunteered its decision timeline unprompted on 23 Jun 2026: final round mid-July, evaluations concluded end of July, and silence in between means nothing.
Six buyer-initiated emails between 16 Jun and 7 Jul 2026 chased Zycus deliverables (demo scheduling, AEC-hosting proposal, partner quotes), all buyer-side pull.

items.action: Confirm with Franck Devienne that Zycus's evaluation file is complete before SAMI's mid-July decision round.
items.trigger: Franck Devienne set a final decision round for mid-July and invited direct email contact at the 23 Jun 2026 demo
items.expected_effect: No open Zycus item enters the decision round unanswered, and the commercial dialogue Franck invited is opened on Zycus's terms.

items.action: Map the SAMI executives who ratify the award and open a first line through Mohamad Alhakim's Riyadh office.
items.trigger: CRM flags an arms-length process with no champion and unconfirmed authority above Franck Devienne
items.expected_effect: First thread above procurement before award ratification, reducing single-thread risk on a $2.2M decision.

items.action: Offer Franck Devienne a live ST Engineering reference call for SAMI's final evaluation round.
items.trigger: Franck Devienne requested detailed A&D OEM usage evidence on 4 Jun 2026 and follow-up clarifications on 10 Jun 2026
items.expected_effect: A&D peer validation at the decision point, converting the written reference list into buyer-heard proof against SAP Ariba and GEP.

items.action: If selected, co-author a mutual close plan with Franck covering SI commercials, InfoSec and contracting dates.
items.trigger: SAMI's evaluations conclude end of July 2026 per its own stated timeline
items.expected_effect: Converts an award into a papered close and surfaces the realistic close date against 29 Aug 2026.

items.action: Stage the defense-grade InfoSec pack and AEC on-premise deployment architecture for immediate post-award contracting.
items.trigger: Franck Devienne classified AEC hosting as on-premise on 16 Jun 2026, adding defense InfoSec depth to any contracting
items.expected_effect: Compresses the legal, InfoSec and signature tracks into the short post-award window instead of discovering them then.

calls_omitted.reason: No transcript or notes available in any store; outcome known only from the dated owner entry of 26 Feb 2026 (positive 4-hour presentation/workshop), so this meeting is unread rather than absent.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d (through 2026-07-07); OpportunityFieldHistory 365d; full Next_Step_History__c Dec 2025-Jul 2026; contact roles; MEDDPICC 2.0
gaps: 24 Feb 2026 presentation content unread (no transcript or notes); its positive outcome is known from the dated owner entry only
Executive layer above Franck Devienne unmapped across all sources
The other two shortlisted finalists are never named in any source
Several 23 Jun 2026 demo questioners (e.g. a speaker addressed as Ali and a local-content colleague) could not be resolved to the contact roster and are treated as unverified SAMI evaluators
